cards.question: What is a Kafka partition and why does it matter?
cards.answer: A partition is an ordered log shard of a topic; it enables parallelism and determines message ordering scope.

cards.question: Difference between at-most-once and at-least-once delivery?
cards.answer: At-most-once may lose messages but avoids duplicates; at-least-once avoids loss but may produce duplicates requiring idempotency.

cards.question: What does consumer group rebalancing do?
cards.answer: Rebalancing redistributes partitions among group members when consumers join/leave, ensuring load sharing and failover.

cards.question: Why use key-based partitioning?
cards.answer: Records with the same key go to the same partition, preserving per-key order for processing logic.

cards.question: What is idempotent producer in Kafka?
cards.answer: Idempotent producer prevents duplicate writes caused by retries by assigning sequence numbers and producer IDs.
